newsletters.subject: Codex Goes Everywhere
newsletters.summary: Covers OpenAI's Codex mobile app launch, enabling phone-based agent management. Key topics: Codex going mobile (ChatGPT app integration for monitoring agents, reviewing outputs, approving commands, changing models, seeing screenshots/terminal output), remote SSH, hooks, programmatic access tokens, HIPAA compliance for enterprise. Peter Steinberger (OpenClaw founder) reportedly helping Codex team. Codex CLI is open source and self-writes ~90% of its own code.

wiki_candidates.note: Peter Steinberger's collaboration with OpenAI Codex team

newsletters.subject: [AINews] Cerebras' $60B IPO: Slowly, then All at Once
newsletters.summary: Covers Cerebras Systems IPO (ticker: CBRS) pricing at $185/share, first-day surge to $350 (+89%), $67B market cap. Largest US tech IPO since 2019. WSE-3 wafer-scale chip architecture as NVIDIA alternative for AI inference. Key deals: OpenAI $20B compute agreement, Amazon partnership. 2025 revenue $510M (+76% YoY), $24.6B remaining performance obligation. Multiclass share structure (A: 1 vote, B: 20 votes insiders, N: non-voting warrants for OpenAI/Amazon). Signals broader AI IPO wave with OpenAI, Anthropic, SpaceX expected next.

wiki_candidates.note: Cerebras positioning vs NVIDIA in inference market
Cerebras as first major AI IPO, precedent for upcoming OpenAI/Anthropic/SpaceX listings
